rulings.Case Title: BRIAN WALTON VS. PATRICK W CONLON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Good Faith Settlement Of X-Deft.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 15, 2010, line 1, CROSS DEFENDANT AUTOQUIP CORPORATION MOTION For Good Faith Settlement of cross-defendant. Motion granted. Settlement comports with the good faith requirement of CCP section 877.6 and satisfies Tech-Bitt factors. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES ENGELBERG et al VS. ELLEN TSANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 15, 2010, line 2, PLAINTIFFS CHARLES ENGELBERG, BECKY ENGELBERG Amended Notice of Motion To Tax Costs. Motion granted in part and denied in part. Motion granted as to $225 for "service of process" for papers to be served on plaintiffs "as soon as possible." Plaintiff meets burden to show costs unreasonable and unnecessary when papers can be served by mail. Motion denied in all other respects. Exhibit photo copies and BASF fee allowed under CCP section 1033.5(c)(4) in court's discretion in view of timing of dismissal. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IRENA ROTBART VS. T-MOBILE USA,INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 15, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT T-MOBILE USA, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th and 7th causes of action due to preemption. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to 6th cause of action for unfair business practices. Plaintiff to plead facts that demonstrate standing and lack of preemption. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IRENA ROTBART VS. T-MOBILE USA,INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 15, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT T-MOBILE USA, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion to strike granted, no opposition. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 15, 2010, line 4, PLAINTIFF ROBERT COPELAND Demurrer To Defendants' Answer To Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint. Off Calendar. Amended Answer filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 15, 2010, line 5, PLAINTIFF STEPHEN GILLETT Demurrer. Off Calendar, case assigned to Judge Munter and he issued a stay of the proceedings. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 15, 2010, line 5, PLAINTIFF STEPHEN GILLETT MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Off Calendar, case assigned to Judge Munter and he issued a stay of the proceedings. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK VS. GUY L KORNBLUM et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 15, 2010, line 6, CROSS DEFENDANT AMERICAN EXPRESS CENTURION BANK DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to CLRA aspect of the cause of action under Berry vs. American Express Publishing, Inc. (2007) 147 Cal.App.4th 224, 227. Body of cross-complaint reflects that suit pertains to extension of credit. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to remaining allegations in the cause of action. Cross-complainant to plead cause(s) of action in compliance with CRC Rule 2.112, separately stating each cause of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MINOTT WESSINGER AS TRUSTEE OF THE MINOTT VS. EDWARD KAVOUNAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice O Mfotion And Motion For Order Granting Access To Plfs' Land To Prevent Serious Harm To Deft'S Property
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 15, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANT EDWARD KAVOUNAS Motion For Order Granting Access To Plaintiffs' Land To Prevent Serious Harm To Defendant's Property. Motion denied. Insufficient evidence of emergency presented for relief under section 197(1)(a) of the Restatement, Second, of Torts. Temporary waterproofing material to remain in place. =(302/CWW)
